title: TOUCH
description: Returns the number of existing keys out of those specified after updating the time they were last accessed.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys that will be touched.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information. Alters the last access time of a key(s). A key is ignored if it does not exist. ## Examples SET key1 "Hello" SET key2 "World" TOUCH key1 key2 ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of touched keys. **RESP3:** [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of touched keys.
intro:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



Alters the last access time of a key(s).
A key is ignored if it does not exist.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



Alters the last access time of a key(s).
A key is ignored if it does not exist.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
SET key1 "Hello"
SET key2 "World"
TOUCH key1 key2

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

| Redis

Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of touched keys.

**RESP3:**

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of touched keys.
